risk_domain.name: Off-chain Signature Phishing
risk_domain.description: Phishing attacks that exploit off-chain signature mechanisms (EIP-712 typed data, EIP-2612 permits, Permit2, Seaport orders) to steal tokens without requiring on-chain approval transactions

capabilities: deploy convincing phishing dApp frontends mimicking legitimate protocols
craft arbitrary EIP-712 typed data payloads for user signing
register lookalike ENS domains and deploy clone contracts
monitor mempool for signed permits and front-run revocations
chain multiple signed permits into a single drain transaction
limitations: cannot forge signatures without private key
cannot modify signed message after signature
cannot bypass EIP-712 domain separator verification in compliant wallets

H1_permit2_long_expiry.name: Permit2 long expiry
H1_permit2_long_expiry.description: Identifies Permit2 transactions with excessively long expiration times, potentially allowing unauthorized access.

signals.name: long_expiration
signals.description: Expiration timestamp set more than 30 days from signing time.

signals.name: unlimited_amount
signals.description: Permit2 transaction with amount greater than or equal to 2^159 (effectively unlimited).

signals.name: unknown_spender
signals.description: Spender address is not in any known protocol registry or verified contract list.

recommendations.description: Block Permit2 transactions with expiration more than 30 days.
Set a maximum allowed amount for Permit2 transactions to 2^159.
Only allow Permit2 transactions from known and verified spender addresses.

H2_eip_712_data_encoding_anomalie.name: EIP-712 data encoding anomalies
H2_eip_712_data_encoding_anomalie.description: Detects unexpected or misleading fields in EIP-712 typed data, indicating possible misrepresentation by the dApp.

signals.name: unexpected_expiration_field
signals.description: Detection of an unexpected expiration field value that is abnormally high or outside the expected range.

signals.name: unverified_spender_address
signals.description: Identification of a spender address not found in any known protocol registry or verified contract list, suggesting potential misrepresentation.

signals.name: excessive_amount_value
signals.description: Detection of an unusually high amount value that could indicate a security risk or misuse of the EIP-712 data.

recommendations.description: Review before proceeding

H3_unverified_operator_approval.name: Unverified operator approval
H3_unverified_operator_approval.description: Flags transactions approving unknown operators that are not from verified marketplaces or protocols.

signals.name: unknown_operator
signals.description: Operator address is not found in any known protocol registry or verified contract list.

signals.name: unverified_marketplace
signals.description: Transaction involves an operator from a non-verified marketplace or platform.

signals.name: long_expiration_timestamp
signals.description: Expiration timestamp of the permit is set significantly longer than typical usage periods (e.g., more than 30 days).

signals.name: inconsistent_approvals
signals.description: Multiple approvals with high amounts and long expirations from unknown operators.

recommendations.description: Block transactions from unknown operators.
Ensure all marketplaces are on a trusted list before use.
Automatically set expiration timestamps to short periods (e.g., 24 hours).
Regularly monitor and review all operator approvals.

H4_unverified_dapp_signature.name: Unverified dApp signature
H4_unverified_dapp_signature.description: Detects signatures coming from unverified domains, increasing the risk of phishing or fraudulent activities.

signals.name: unverified_domain_signature
signals.description: Signature domain is not verified or recognized by known security lists.

signals.name: uncommon_signature_extension
signals.description: Signature uses uncommon or less secure extensions that are not widely adopted.

signals.name: outdated_signature_timestamp
signals.description: Signature timestamp is far in the future, possibly indicating delayed execution risks.

recommendations.description: Block transactions or interactions from unverified domains.
Manually verify the domain before proceeding with any actions.
Automatically update your security lists to include verified domains.
Disable or block transactions using uncommon signature extensions.
Assess and ignore signatures with outdated timestamps.

H5_batch_permit_with_multiple_tok.name: Batch permit with multiple tokens
H5_batch_permit_with_multiple_tok.description: Identifies Permit2 batch transactions that involve multiple token approvals, which may increase risk due to higher complexity.

signals.name: multiple_token_approvals
signals.description: Identifies transactions involving multiple token approvals within a Permit2 batch.

signals.name: high_approval_amounts
signals.description: Detects large approval amounts in Permit2 batch transactions, indicating higher risk.

signals.name: long_expiration_times
signals.description: Identifies permits with expiration timestamps set far into the future, suggesting increased risk.

recommendations.description: Limit the number of tokens approved in batch transactions.
Set up alerts for high approval amounts.
Shorten the expiration time for permits.
Automate monitoring and alerting using a security tool.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Test Batch permit with multiple tokens

H6_seaport_order_undervaluation.name: Seaport order undervaluation
H6_seaport_order_undervaluation.description: Flags Seaport orders with significantly lower listing prices than the market value of the assets, potentially indicating fraudulent behavior.

signals.name: seaport_order_price_mismatch
signals.description: Price of the seaport order is significantly lower than the estimated market value of the assets being sold.

signals.name: seaport_order_volume_mismatch
signals.description: Volume of the seaport order is disproportionately low relative to typical market trades involving similar assets.

signals.name: seaport_order_frequency
signals.description: Frequency of seaport orders by a single user is unusually high within a short time frame, possibly indicating manipulative behavior.

recommendations.description: Automate monitoring for seaport orders below market value.
Manually verify the asset's estimated market value before listing.
Automatically limit the volume of low-priced orders to prevent manipulation.
Automate tracking and alerting for frequent, suspiciously low-price listings.

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring off-chain signature phishing detection across all vectors

methodology.step_1: Collect dataset of known phishing signatures from Scam Sniffer, Forta alerts, and wallet drainer kit analysis
methodology.step_2: Collect dataset of legitimate off-chain signatures from DeFi protocols (Uniswap, OpenSea, 1inch, Cowswap)
methodology.step_3: Run analyzer against both sets with all 6 heuristics
methodology.step_4: Measure: true positive rate (phishing caught), false positive rate (legitimate flagged)
methodology.step_5: Apply recommendations and re-measure user exposure

combined_benchmark.target: Catch >90% of known phishing signatures with <5% false positive rate on legitimate DeFi

adversary_levels.name: basic
adversary_levels.description: Unlimited permits + NFT approval to unknown operator

adversary_levels.name: intermediate
adversary_levels.description: Add disguised EIP-712 transfers + unverified origin

adversary_levels.name: advanced
adversary_levels.description: All heuristics including batch drain and Seaport manipulation

templates.summary: Analyzed {n_sigs} off-chain signature requests. {n_risky} flagged ({pct_risky}%). Value at risk: ${value_at_risk}. Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}: {skill_action}. Reduces phishing risk by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
